authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: Clear Creek — GNIS Feature ID 859255, Carson City, Nevada (USGS Geographic Names Information System mirror)
sources.quotedText: Clear Creek, GNIS feature ID 859255, Carson City (city) County, Nevada. Latitude: 39.0962981, Longitude: -119.7324008. Feature type: Stream.

sources.title: Clear Creek (Nevada) — Wikipedia
sources.quotedText: Clear Creek is a 13.5-mile-long (21.7 km) stream ... It begins at 8,780 feet (2,680 m) on the southern slopes of Snow Valley Peak ... in the Carson Range ... It is the only perennial tributary of the Carson River mainstem ... Its mouth is at its confluence with the Carson River in the southeast portion of Carson City, Nevada, at 39°05′47″N 119°43′57″W.

sources.title: Clear Creek — Carson City — Nevada Department of Wildlife (dedicated water profile)
sources.quotedText: Region: Western. County: Carson City. Type of water: Creek or Stream.

sources.title: Clear Creek Sub-Watershed — Carson Water Subconservancy District
sources.quotedText: Clear Creek is a year-round tributary of the Carson River located east of Lake Tahoe and south of Carson City. The Clear Creek Watershed comprises nearly 14 square miles and flows from the Carson Range near Spooner Summit (8,780 feet) to the Carson River (4,639 feet).

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Carson City, general "All waters" listing
sources.quotedText: CARSON CITY. CLOSED WATERS: None. OPEN WATERS: All waters. (Except as noted below). Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye. Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Carson City special-water listings (only 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' and 'Baily Fishing Pond' are broken out; Clear Creek is not named and does not flow into Lake Tahoe)
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset. Baily Fishing Pond. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours park is open to public use.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Clear Creek (GNIS ID 859255) rises on the southern slopes of Snow Valley Peak in the Carson Range, at the edge of the Lake Tahoe Basin, then flows south and east through southern Carson City to its confluence with the Carson River — it is the only perennial tributary of the Carson River mainstem. It does not drain into Lake Tahoe itself, so it is not covered by the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' special row, and it is not the separately-listed Baily Fishing Pond. Carson City has no closed waters, and Clear Creek is not individually listed in CR 25-16, so the governing rule is the county's general 'All waters (Except as noted below)' listing — open year around.

regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Carson City general 'All waters' listing; Clear Creek is not a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not individually listed in CR 25-16).
regulations.verbatimText: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Carson City general limit: Daily 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye); Possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye). On a small trout creek the practical limit is 5 trout per day / 10 in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily / 10 in possession (plus the county's separate mountain-whitefish and warmwater allowances) under the Carson City general 'All waters' limit.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

parameters.note: Under the Carson City general 'All waters' listing, fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night (the restricted 1-hour-before-sunrise-to-2-hours-after-sunset window applies only to the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row, which does not cover Clear Creek).

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Clear Creek is a Nevada (Carson City) inland stream, so a standard Nevada resident/nonresident license applies.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
